fp16.description: Planning class for router/gate tensors that must not be aggressively quantized without evidence.

q8_planning.description: Planning Q8 class with one FP16-like scale per 32 weights.

iq2_planning.description: Aggressive low-bit expert-only planning class; quality is unresolved.

static_weight_groups.name: router_gate_mirrors
static_weight_groups.notes: Router/gate mirrors are replicated for correctness and future B/C local-router validation.

static_weight_groups.name: non_expert_dense_minus_router
static_weight_groups.notes: Planning bucket for attention, embeddings/lm_head if present, norms, and other non-expert tensors.

static_weight_groups.name: moe_experts
static_weight_groups.notes: Expert tensor bucket derived from total versus activated parameter counts and top-8/128 routing.

unresolved_assumptions: Three-node static placement is a planning scenario; if Node A remains control-only, B/C static fractions must be rerun and may fail the cap.
The dense/shared versus expert split is algebraically inferred from 235B total, 22B activated, and 8-of-128 experts rather than real tensor metadata.
The iq2_planning class is a feasibility placeholder, not an accepted quantization recipe.
